data.content_ref: inline:{"analogy":{"adopted_commitment_refs":[],"disanalogies":["TAL has machine-checked proofs; the bridge assertion is runtime-only","Downgrading attacks are usually malicious; this is emergent from incomplete implementation","Compiler TAL has a single target; MiniReason must serve CLI/MCP/chat with different trust models"],"overturn_conditions":["A formal proof exists that the bridge assertion implies MiniReason's forward-compatible parsing is sound","The bridge record schema is proven closed (no extension paths) and MiniReason handles all cases","ops.py audit shows zero bridge-related operations in CLI/MCP paths"],"shared_structure":["A high-assurance component (bridge/TAL) makes strong claims about invariants","A lower-assurance but portable component (MiniReason/target code generator) handles the same data","The interface between them uses a 'forward compatible' or 'partial' representation","The strong invariant is not reified in the lower-assurance component's type system","New consumers of the lower-assurance component inherit the unchecked gap"],"source_memory_refs":["compiler-design: typed-assembly-language-with-unverified-conversion","security: downgrading-attack-on-forward-compatible-protocols"],"transfer_claims":["The 'partially verified decompiler' pattern: MiniReason needs a validation oracle for bridge records that it cannot itself provide, suggesting a harness-side proof witness rather than assertion","The 'capability attenuation' pattern: ops.py should expose bridge operations only through a MiniReason-validated wrapper, not directly","The 'completeness-preserving migration' pattern: extend MiniReason's primitive set before adding new bridge record types, with explicit 'unknown field rejection' as default"]},"claim":"The grounded bridge (src/deepreason/bridge/harness.py:286-457) and MiniReason primitive reuse (mini/minireason/advisory.py:1-120) form a type-soundness gap where the bridge's 'assert formal state is unchanged' postcondition cannot be validated by MiniReason's incomplete forward-compatible handling, creating a verification hole for C6 (local typed bounded repair) and C12 (primitive reuse) that will expand as CLI/MCP/chat integration surfaces multiply.","counterconditions":[{"case":"MiniReason's incomplete primitive reuse is actually complete for all bridge record types currently generated","eval":"observation"},{"case":"The bridge assertion is accompanied by a cryptographic digest check that MiniReason can verify","eval":"observation"},{"case":"ops.py:1-62 does not expose bridge operations to CLI/MCP\u2014only profile gates and seeding","eval":"observation"},{"case":"The forward-compatibility flag in advisory.py causes hard failure on unknown fields, not silent acceptance","eval":"observation"}],"definitions":[],"derivation":[],"formalization_refs":[],"mechanism":"The bridge implements a typed two-stage workflow: freeze formal state, assemble evidence, commit scratch provenance separately, run workflow, assert formal state unchanged. MiniReason's advisory.py has forward-compatible scratch/bridge record handling but incomplete primitive reuse. The gap: the bridge's assertion is a runtime check in Python (bridge/harness.py:286-457), not a proven invariant. MiniReason cannot import this assertion because: (a) MiniReason's type system lacks the bridge's state-frozenness token, (b) the 'incomplete primitive reuse' means bridge record parsing may silently drop fields that encode the assertion, (c) the forward-compatibility flag means MiniReason accepts records it doesn't fully understand rather than rejecting them. For C6, this means repair bounds cannot be verified as local because the bridge's unchanged-formal-state guarantee might be spuriously true (formal state changed in a way the assertion misses). For C12, MiniReason primitives for bridge handling are not actually reused\u2014they're stubbed forward-compatible. For C13 (shared CLI/MCP/chat services), each new surface that invokes MiniReason for lightweight reasoning inherits this unverified gap. The migration risk is that ops.py:1-62's shared application operations become a covert channel for unverified bridge assertions into operator-facing tools.","premises":[],"schema":"deepreason-reasoning-envelope-v1","scope":{"covers":[],"excludes":[]},"simulation_refs":[],"uncertainties":[]}
